key_insight: WarpagePINN predicts chiplet temperature and thermal warpage with a two-stage physics-informed neural network that avoids labeled simulation data by enforcing governing physics directly.
engineering_relevance: It offers a fast, accurate alternative to finite-element thermal-warpage analysis for advanced packaging, helping assess CTE-driven deformation in heterogeneous chiplet assemblies.
method: Stage 1: Fourier-series temperature model trained only with PDE-based loss and built-in boundary conditions.
Stage 2: MLP warpage model trained with a hybrid supervisory strategy using energy-based loss.
Parametric extension: models CTE uncertainty for rapid parameter studies.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong agreement with finite element results, achieving about 0.2 μm MAE and roughly 1000× speedup in CTE parameterization studies.
watch_item: Physics-informed training can be sensitive to loss balancing and parameterization choices, so careful calibration is needed before deployment on new package designs.
paper_title: WarpagePINN: Thermal Warpage Prediction in Advanced Packaging via a Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Networks
